truth.papers.A47.title: High-strength Fe–20Mn–Al–C-based Alloys with Low Density
truth.papers.A47.paper_scope: Investigation of mechanical properties and microstructures of Fe–20Mn–(10–14)Al–(0–1.8)C quaternary and Fe–20Mn–(10–14)Al–(0.75–1.8)C–5Cr quinary alloys for low-density, high-specific-strength steels, including effects of Al, C, Cr contents, cooling rate, and cold rolling.

prediction.papers.A47.title: High-strength Fe–20Mn–Al–C-based Alloys with Low Density
prediction.papers.A47.paper_scope: Investigation of mechanical properties, density and microstructure of Fe-20Mn-Al-C and Fe-20Mn-Al-C-5Cr low-density alloys, focusing on specific strength and κ-carbide precipitation hardening through control of composition and cooling rate.

truth.structures.sample_fe20mn10al15c5cr_ac110: fine and uniform, formed during air cooling

prediction.structures.sample_fe20mn10al15c5cr_wq110: γ matrix with very fine nano-size κ precipitates
very fine precipitates slightly confirmed by dark field imaging of (100)κ superlattice reflection
prediction.structures.sample_fe20mn10al15c5cr_ac110: γ matrix with cuboidal κ precipitates aligned along <001>
cuboidal precipitates aligned along <001> directions; superlattice reflections much stronger than WQ

truth.interfaces.sample_fe20mn10al15c5cr_ac110: likely coherent (based on crystal structure similarity and cuboidal morphology)
Upper yield point followed by nearly constant flow stress attributed to shearing of κ precipitates.
κ phase precipitates during cooling from annealing temperature, with slower cooling favoring more intense superlattice reflections and larger volume fraction.

prediction.interfaces.sample_fe20mn10al15c5cr_ac110: κ-carbide precipitates during cooling from annealing temperature; cuboidal morphology aligned along <001> in air-cooled specimen suggests crystallographic alignment with γ matrix analogous to Ni-based superalloys γ/γ'

truth.performance.sample_fe20mn11al18c5cr_wq11: conventional austenitic, ferritic, martensitic, precipitation hardened, dual-phase, super toughness steels, TWIP steels
higher specific strength than conventional steels, e.g., Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.8C-5Cr showed specific strength over 180 MPa·cm3/g with elongation around 30%

prediction.performance.sample_fe20mn11al18c5cr_wq11: conventional austenitic steels and Fe-Mn-Si-Al TWIP steels

truth.characterization_methods.sample_fe20mn10: microstructure of Fe-20Mn-10Al-0.5C quaternary alloy after 1100°C/15min WQ
truth.characterization_methods.sample_fe20mn11: no stress-induced martensite phase observed in tensile deformed specimen
truth.characterization_methods.sample_fe20mn10: very fine κ phase precipitates slightly confirmed in water-quenched condition; dark field imaging shows weak superlattice spots
cuboidal κ phase aligned along <001> directions clearly observed; stronger superlattice reflections indicate higher volume fraction of κ during air cooling

prediction.characterization_methods.idx_0.purpose: Phase identification and microstructure observation at room temperature
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_0.key_findi: Identified γ, γ+α, γ+κ and α phases in Fe-Mn-Al-C(-Cr) alloys annealed at 1100°C; confirmed γ single phase and γ+κ/γ+α two-phase regions
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe20mn10: Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) and selected area diffraction
Observation of κ-carbide precipitation in water-quenched specimen
Very fine κ precipitates slightly confirmed; extra spots between fundamental reflections indicating perovskite structure; intensity of superlattice reflections lower than air-cooled
Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) and dark field imaging
Observation of κ-carbide precipitation in air-cooled specimen
Cuboidal κ phase precipitates aligned along <001> directions clearly observed in dark field of (100)κ superlattice reflection; superlattice reflections intensity much higher than water-quenched
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_3.key_findi: Hardness increased with increasing Al and C contents; air-cooled specimens exhibited higher hardness than water-quenched due to κ precipitation
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_4.key_findi: Yield strength, tensile strength and elongation measured at strain rate 0.5 mm/min using dumbbell-shaped specimens; specific strength calculated from tensile strength and density
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe20mn11: Check for stress-induced martensite after tensile deformation
No stress-induced martensite phase detected in tensile deformed specimen

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: calculated density agrees well with experimental values from Archimedes method, e.g., Fe-20Mn-10Al-5Cr-1.5C alloy: calculated 6.64, observed 6.67 g/cm3
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: linear mixing rule for fcc density
D_A^fcc = sum(D_i^fcc * x_i), where x_i is atomic fraction. Parameters estimated from lattice constants of binary fcc alloys.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Calculated density agreed with Archimedes measurement, e.g., calculated 6.64 vs observed 6.67 g/cm3 for Fe-20Mn-10Al-5Cr-1.5C alloy
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Estimation of alloy density from pure element fcc density parameters and atomic fractions
Linear mixture rule using atomic fractions; density parameters evaluated by extrapolation from binary fcc alloys; applicable to γ single phase and extended to other phases
prediction.computational_details.idx_1.validation_a: Calculated equilibrium phase diagram of Fe-20Mn-Al-C-5Cr at 1100°C agreed with OM observation results

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Cold-workability evaluated by parameter W = (t0 - tmin)/t0 x 100% during cold-rolling at room temperature.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Figure 2(a) indicates specific cold-workability values for quaternary alloys: Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.5C (γ) hardness 466 HV, W=74%; Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.2C (γ+α) 302 HV, W>90%; Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.0C (γ) 295 HV, W>90%; Fe-20Mn-10Al-0.5C (γ+α) 270 HV, W=88%; Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.8C (γ+κ) 413 HV, W=0%; etc.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.8C-5Cr γ alloy after 1100°C WQ showed specific strength of 183 MPa·cm3/g with elongation 29.0%, and after 30% cold rolling specific strength over 230 MPa·cm3/g.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Cold-rolled Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.5C (93% reduction) achieved hardness 681 HV, yield strength 1970 MPa, UTS 2111 MPa, specific strength 323 MPa·cm3/g.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Fe-20Mn-13Al-1.3C-5Cr γ+α alloy after 1100°C AC showed specific strength about 199 MPa·cm3/g.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Comparison in Fig.8 shows present alloys have higher specific strength than austenitic, ferritic, martensitic, precipitation hardened, dual-phase, super toughness steels and TWIP steels; TRIP steels can be higher but require complex thermomechanical processing.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Density of Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.8C-5Cr alloy is 6.51 g/cm3, with specific strength exceeding 180 MPa·cm3/g and tensile elongation around 40% (air cooled condition).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Cooling rate effect: water quenching ~1100°C/s, air cooling ~15°C/s.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Fig. 4 provides typical stress–strain curves for quaternary and quinary alloys; exact numerical stress–strain pairs beyond approximate figure annotations are not provided in the text and require image pixel access.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Cold-workability parameter W was defined as W = (t0 - tmin)/t0 × 100%, where t0 is initial thickness and tmin is minimum thickness before crack appearance during room-temperature cold-rolling.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Fig. 8 presents a comparative plot of specific strength versus tensile elongation for the present alloys against conventional austenitic, ferritic, martensitic, precipitation hardened, dual-phase, super toughness and Fe-Mn-Si-Al TWIP steels.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: The present Fe-Mn-Al-C(-Cr) alloys exhibit higher specific strength than conventional steels and TWIP steels, though TRIP steels reported by Zakay et al. possess higher specific strength via complicated thermomechanical processing.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Density parameters used in estimation: D_Fe^fcc = 8.19, D_Mn^fcc = 7.19, D_Al^fcc = 2.70, D_Cr^fcc = 6.36, D_C^fcc = 2.85 (g/cm3).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Hot-rolling was performed at 1200°C to sheets of about 1.5 mm thickness prior to heat treatment and mechanical testing.
